steps.title: Recognize the unexpected outcome
steps.content: The principle predicts that rings outside the Roche limit accrete into satellites, but Morgado's team found a ring outside this limit that remained intact—the opposite of what should happen.

steps.title: Pick the transition for surprise findings
steps.content:
The logical relationship is concession/contrast — the principle predicts the ring should accrete into a satellite, but the finding contradicts that expectation. The blank must flag this contradiction.
  • Choice A — likewise,: signals that Morgado's finding parallels or confirms the principle; it does the opposite, so this frames the finding incorrectly. ✗
  • Choice B — fittingly,: signals that the finding appropriately matches expectations; but the finding defies expectations, making "fittingly" misleading. ✗
  • Choice C — for example,: introduces a specific instance that supports a general claim; the finding does not support the principle, it contradicts it. ✗
  • Choice D — though,: signals a contrasting or unexpected result, marking that the ring outside the Roche limit unexpectedly remained intact rather than accreting. ✓
D is correct.
